name: swarm-review
description: Streaming-mode variant of review.json — bugs/perf start the instant scan resolves instead of waiting for a shared batch level, a conditional edge skips the whole review when nothing changed, a generator step can spin up extra reviewers for a large changeset, and a run-wide token budget caps total spend. Run with: carina workflow run swarm-review. See protocol/schemas/workflow-graph.schema.json for every field's exact meaning.

steps.task: List the files changed in the working tree. Finish with a JSON object: {"files": ["path1", "path2", ...], "count": <number of files>}.
Review the given files for correctness bugs.
Review the given files for performance issues. If "bugs" publishes anything to the "review-progress" channel while you work, take it into account.

If scan.count is greater than 20, use spawn_steps to add one additional reviewer step per extra 20 files, each reviewing a distinct slice of scan.files for style and test coverage. If scan.count is 20 or fewer, finish with an empty spawn_steps list — nothing to add.
